name: Stencil
description: A compiler for generating standards-compliant Web Components that work across all major frameworks

description.value: A compiler for generating standards-compliant Web Components that work across all major frameworks

initial-release.comment: Created by the Ionic team to build faster, more capable components for Ionic Framework v4

active-maintenance.comment: Actively maintained with 209 releases, 245 contributors, regular releases throughout 2025

reactivity-model.comment: Uses Virtual DOM with async rendering capabilities for efficient updates

rendering-paradigm.comment: Declarative JSX/TSX templates with render() method returning DOM structure

state-management-approach.comment: @State decorator for local state; @stencil/store for shared state; Redux also supported

opinionatedness.comment: Provides structure via decorators and conventions but allows flexibility in integration

backward-compatibility.comment: Maintains compatibility; major versions include migration guides

primary-language.comment: Components written in TypeScript with JSX; compiles to standard Web Components

compiles-to.comment: Compiles TypeScript/JSX to standards-compliant JavaScript Web Components

typescript-support.comment: Built with TypeScript as the primary language; decorators for component definition

template-syntax.comment: Uses TSX (TypeScript JSX) for component templates in render() method

styling-approach.comment: Supports Shadow DOM for style encapsulation; scoped CSS option available

build-required.comment: Stencil is a compiler; build step required to generate Web Components

reactive-primitives.comment: @State decorator marks class members as reactive; changes trigger re-renders

computed-values.comment: No built-in computed properties; use getters or @stencil/store onChange handlers

two-way-binding.comment: No built-in two-way binding; uses event-based pattern for form inputs

event-handling.comment: @Event decorator for custom events; standard event binding in JSX

conditional-rendering.comment: Standard JSX ternary operators and conditional expressions in render()

list-rendering.comment: Array.map() in JSX for list rendering

component-slots.comment: Native <slot> element support via Shadow DOM and Web Components standard

teleport-portal.comment: No built-in portal; can use native DOM APIs

suspense.comment: No built-in Suspense; async rendering handled via lifecycle methods

error-boundaries.comment: No built-in error boundaries; handle errors in application code

context-provide-inject.comment: No built-in context; use @stencil/store or events for cross-component communication

refs-direct-dom-access.comment: @Element decorator provides reference to host element; can query shadow root

official-router.comment: @stencil/router provided but deprecated; wiki last updated 2018; community recommends alternatives

router-name.comment: Official router exists but not actively maintained; nested routes marked 'coming soon' since 2018

nested-routes.comment: Nested routes were listed as 'coming soon' in @stencil/router wiki (2018)

lazy-loading-routes.comment: Lazy loading is default behavior in Stencil; components loaded on demand

built-in-state.comment: @State decorator for internal reactive component state

built-in-global-state.comment: @stencil/store provides official lightweight shared state solution

global-state-library.comment: Lightweight key/value store by Stencil core team with automatic re-rendering

devtools-state-inspection.comment: Stencil Inspector Chrome extension for debugging; standard Web Components DevTools

state-persistence.comment: No built-in persistence; implement via localStorage or third-party

time-travel-debugging.comment: Not built into @stencil/store; available via Redux integration

official-cli.comment: create-stencil CLI: npm init stencil for project scaffolding with templates (component, app, ionic-pwa)

hot-module-replacement.comment: Built-in live reload and small dev server in the compiler

browser-devtools.comment: Stencil Inspector Chrome extension; also standard Web Components DevTools

vs-code-extension.comment: Stencil Tools extension for VS Code; also docs-vscode output target for custom element info

error-messages.comment: Clear errors with component context from TypeScript compiler

documentation-quality.comment: Good documentation with guides, API reference, examples, FAQ, and framework integration guides

interactive-tutorial.comment: No official interactive tutorial; getting started guide and examples available

playground.comment: No official online playground; recommend local development with CLI

bundle-size-core.comment: Stencil is a compiler, not a runtime. Generated components have minimal runtime overhead; lazy loading is default

bundle-size-full.comment: Bundle size depends on components used; compiler performs static analysis to include only what's needed per component

virtual-dom.comment: Uses Virtual DOM with async rendering for efficient updates

compile-time-optimization.comment: Compiler performs static analysis to understand API usage and optimize each component's runtime

tree-shaking.comment: dist-custom-elements output is tree-shakable; uses Rollup for bundling

code-splitting.comment: Automatic code-splitting via lazy loading; only imports components used on page

lazy-components.comment: Lazy loading is default; components loaded on-demand as they appear in DOM

ssr-capable.comment: dist-hydrate-script output target for SSR; integrates with Next.js, Nuxt, etc.

streaming-ssr.comment: streamToString() returns Readable stream for streaming SSR to HTTP responses

partial-hydration.comment: Components asynchronously hydrate in DOM order; each component lazily hydrates and reuses existing nodes

js-framework-benchmark.comment: Stencil is included in js-framework-benchmark but specific results vary by version

npm-weekly-downloads.comment: ~800K weekly downloads for @stencil/core as of December 2025; ~3M monthly

component-libraries.comment: Ionic Framework, Shoelace, Calcite Design System, Porsche Design System, State of Michigan Design System

meta-framework.comment: No dedicated meta-framework; SSR/SSG capabilities built-in; integrates with Next.js, Nuxt, etc.

major-companies-using.value: Apple, Amazon, Porsche, Volkswagen, MasterCard, Upwork, State of Michigan
major-companies-using.comment: Apple Music, Apple TV, Amazon Music built with Stencil; Porsche and VW design systems

official-sponsor-backer.comment: Created and maintained by Ionic; recently transitioned to community-driven governance

discord-community-size.comment: Ionic Discord has ~11,051 members; includes Stencil channel; dedicated Stencil Discord also launched

stack-overflow-questions.comment: Estimated based on stenciljs tag activity

job-market-demand.comment: Growing niche; valued for enterprise design systems; often paired with Ionic Framework

official-testing-library.comment: Built-in testing support with Stencil Test Runner, Jest, and Puppeteer integration

testing-library-name.comment: Built-in test runner based on Jest 27-29 with Puppeteer for browser-based testing

component-testing.comment: Unit testing with spec tests for testing component methods in isolation

e2e-testing-integration.comment: E2E tests with Puppeteer, WebdriverIO, or Playwright support

snapshot-testing.comment: Jest snapshot testing supported via built-in test runner

mobile-framework.comment: Stencil powers Ionic Framework; Capacitor provides native mobile deployment

pwa-support.comment: Built-in service worker generation via Workbox; ionic-pwa starter template available

desktop-framework.comment: Web components work in Electron; no official desktop-specific tooling

learning-curve.comment: Moderate; requires understanding TypeScript, decorators, Web Components concepts, and JSX

concepts-to-learn.comment: Web Components, Shadow DOM, Decorators (@Component, @Prop, @State, @Event), JSX, Lifecycle, Build system

time-to-hello-world.comment: ~3 minutes with npm init stencil; scaffolds project with example component

migration-path.comment: Migration guides available for major version upgrades

incremental-adoption.comment: Generated Web Components work anywhere HTML works; can be used in React, Angular, Vue, or vanilla JS
